# Sirr.Client — Sirr .NET SDK

.NET HTTP client for the Sirr ephemeral secret vault API.
Published to NuGet as `Sirr.Client`. Async-first, built on HttpClient.
Targets .NET 8+.

## Install

dotnet add package Sirr.Client

## Usage

using Sirr.Client;

var client = new SirrClient(new SirrOptions
{
    Server = "http://localhost:39999",
    Token = "your-key"
});

await client.PushAsync("DB_PASSWORD", "s3cr3t", ttl: TimeSpan.FromHours(1), reads: 1);
string? value = await client.GetAsync("DB_PASSWORD");   // null if burned/expired
await client.DeleteAsync("DB_PASSWORD");
var secrets = await client.ListAsync();
var all = await client.PullAllAsync();    // IReadOnlyDictionary<string, string>
int pruned = await client.PruneAsync();

## Errors

- GetAsync returns null on 404 (burned/expired) — never throws for not-found
- All other non-2xx responses throw SirrException

## Dependency Injection

services.AddSingleton<SirrClient>(sp =>
    new SirrClient(new SirrOptions { Server = "...", Token = "..." }));

## Source

github.com/sirrlock/dotnet
